prerequisites: An SFTP server reachable over the network. SFTP runs as a subsystem of SSH, so the server must accept SSH connections (default TCP port 22).
Network access from where pm runs to the SFTP server's host and port. Provide the host and, if it does not listen on the default port 22, the port.
An account on the SFTP server (username) that is authorized to log in and read the files you want to sync.
Read access to the directory you intend to sync. Optionally scope the sync to a subdirectory using folder_path.
Credentials for that account: either the account password or an SSH private key whose matching public key is registered for the account on the server.

Knowledge of the file streams to read (the streams config), including each file's format. If a stream uses document processing that calls an external parsing service, an API key for that service (streams[].format.processing.api_key).

authMethods.name: Password authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to the SSH/SFTP server with the account password. Set username and supply credentials.password. SSH defines this as the 'password' method, where the client sends the password over the already-encrypted SSH transport (RFC 4252).

authMethods.name: SSH public-key (private key) authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an SSH key pair instead of a password. Supply the account username and the SSH private key in credentials.private_key; the matching public key must be installed for that account on the server. SSH defines this as the 'publickey' method, where the client proves possession of the private key via a signature (RFC 4252).

setupSteps.title: Identify the SFTP server and account
setupSteps.body: Get the server's hostname or IP and the SSH/SFTP port. SFTP is an SSH subsystem, so it uses the SSH port, which defaults to 22; set the port config field only if the server uses a non-default port. Confirm the username of the account you will connect as.

setupSteps.title: Choose and prepare credentials
setupSteps.body: Decide between password or SSH key authentication. For password auth, obtain the account password for credentials.password. For key auth, generate or obtain an SSH key pair, ensure the public key is registered for the account on the server, and provide the private key in credentials.private_key. Keep these values in pm's secret store.

setupSteps.title: Set the connection and credentials config
setupSteps.body: Configure host, port (if non-default), and username, then set the credentials object with either the password or the private key for the method you chose. The credentials, host, username, and streams fields are required.

setupSteps.title: Scope the files and streams to sync
setupSteps.body: Optionally set folder_path to restrict syncing to a specific directory on the server, and start_date to only pick up files at or after a given time. Define the streams to read, including each stream's file format and parsing options. If a stream's format uses document processing backed by an external parsing service, supply that service's key in streams[].format.processing.api_key.

setupSteps.title: Configure delivery and run
setupSteps.body: Optionally set delivery_method to control how matched files are delivered, then run the connector. SFTP delegates security to the SSH transport, so a successful run depends on the server accepting your username with the chosen password or public key.

sources.title: SSH File Transfer Protocol (draft-ietf-secsh-filexfer-02): runs as an SSH subsystem over a secure, already-authenticated channel
RFC 4252 - The Secure Shell (SSH) Authentication Protocol: password and publickey methods
SSH File Transfer Protocol - Wikipedia: default port 22, runs over SSH, relies on SSH authentication (password and public key)
